decisions.title: Adopt file-based feature flag system for Sift Edge deployment
decisions.context: ENG-10662 proposes migrating feature flags to file-based system. Critical for Sift Edge (strategic product) to operate offline and in edge environments. Aligns with 'Build the Moat' pillar.

arguments.for: Enables offline operation for edge deployments, reduces cloud dependencies, improves resilience for industrial customers, supports 'Embed Deeper' pillar
arguments.against: Increases configuration complexity, potential drift between cloud and edge configs, requires new deployment tooling

decisions.title: Prioritize Windows SQLite compatibility fix for Sift Edge
decisions.context: ENG-10665 blocks Sift Edge on Windows environments. Many industrial/manufacturing customers use Windows. Critical for 'Embed Deeper' strategy and customer expansion.
decisions.blockedBy: Needs root cause analysis on Windows file locking behavior with SQLite

arguments.for: Unblocks Windows deployments, large TAM in manufacturing/industrial on Windows, quick fix with high customer impact
arguments.against: May require architectural changes to storage layer, could delay other Edge features

decisions.title: Launch Artifacts RFC for cross-product context sharing
decisions.context: ENG-10663 Artifacts RFC could enable sharing context between Canvas, Explore V2, and Python Rules. Supports 'Nail the Core' by improving product cohesion and decisions captured per customer.

arguments.for: Unifies product experience, enables Signal>Evidence>Decision flow across tools, increases stickiness, supports North Star metric
arguments.against: Significant engineering investment, potential scope creep, requires cross-team coordination

decisions.title: Accelerate CEL syntax highlighting rollout across platform
decisions.context: ENG-10519 adds CEL shiki support. CEL is foundation for Python Rules and Canvas expressions. Better DX increases adoption and decisions captured.

arguments.for: Low effort, high impact on user experience, reduces errors, supports Python Rules adoption
arguments.against: Not a blocker for core functionality, resources might be better spent on features

decisions.title: Commit to Starpath and Longshot partnership go-to-market execution
decisions.context: CAGR-37, DES-172, DES-173 indicate partnership announcements with aerospace customers. Aligns with 'Build the Moat' through strategic customer relationships and vertical dominance.

arguments.for: Validates product in high-value vertical, creates reference architecture, generates pipeline, builds competitive moat
arguments.against: Requires sales/marketing resources, potential over-customization for specific verticals

decisions.title: Resource usage optimization sprint for infrastructure efficiency
decisions.context: ENG-10671, ENG-10666, ENG-10667 indicate performance reviews needed. As we scale 'Embed Deeper', infrastructure efficiency impacts margins and customer performance.

arguments.for: Reduces operational costs, improves customer performance, enables larger deployments, supports enterprise scalability
arguments.against: Diverts resources from feature development, benefits may take time to materialize

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy meeting load this week (80% scheduled). Three 1:1s today suggest people need unblocking. Limited focus time (2hrs) risks decision quality.
alerts: Meeting load 80% - consider canceling recurring meetings
Only 2 focus blocks today - decisions need deep work time

Week pattern: reactive vs. strategic time imbalance
